breakClass: left-join null member access / non-nullable value-column read on a possibly-null DefaultIfEmpty() entity in a server-side projection (EF Core 10 shaper reads SQL NULL into a non-nullable CLR value type â€” decimal/int/DateTime/enum â€” and throws; EF6 silently produced default(T))
why: Restaurant-order variant of OrderController.Update; identical SEPayments left-join 500 risk. Legacy identical.
finalFix:
In QuickNetCore/Controllers/OrderRESController.cs (method `salesData`, the `else`/non-Walking branch), change lines 1557-1558 from:
    SEPaidAmount = d.SEPaidAmount,
    SEDueAmount = a.SEGrandTotal - d.SEPaidAmount,
to:
    SEPaidAmount = d != null ? d.SEPaidAmount : 0,
    SEDueAmount = a.SEGrandTotal - (d != null ? d.SEPaidAmount : 0),
This mirrors legacy EF6 behavior (which produced 0 for a NULL left-join member) and is output-preserving. The same minimal fix should be applied to the byte-identical sibling at QuickNetCore/Controllers/OrderController.cs:915-916.
reasoning: I could NOT refute this; it is a genuine EF Core 10 port-break. Verified facts: (1) `d` is a true left-join nullable entity â€” `OrderRESController.cs:1539-1540` `join d in db.SEPayments on a.SalesEntryId equals d.SalesEntry into pay / from d in pay.DefaultIfEmpty()`, so `d` can be SQL NULL. (2) `SEPayment.SEPaidAmount` is a NON-nullable `decimal` (Models\SalesEntry.cs:404). Reading SQL NULL into a non-nullable decimal in the EF Core 10 shaper throws InvalidOperationException -> HTTP 500; EF6 silently yielded default(decimal)=0. (3) Server-side: the query spans lines 1534-1589 and is terminated by `.FirstOrDefault()` with NO `.ToList()`/`.AsEnumerable()` boundary before it; lines 1557 (`SEPaidAmount = d.SEPaidAmount`) and 1558 (`SEDueAmount = a.SEGrandTotal - d.SEPaidAmount`) are in the top-level `select new`, translated to SQL â€” confirmed server-side. (4) Port regression: legacy `_archive\legacy-mvc5\Controllers\OrderRESController.cs:1695-1696` is byte-identical â€” faithful port, broke only due to EF Core 10 null-read semantics. (5) Realistic NULL path exists: the POS save path at lines 333-336 does `RemoveRange(existing SEPayments)` + `SaveChanges()` BEFORE re-adding a SEPayment at line 397, so a SalesEntry can exist with zero SEPayment rows (orphaned/partial-save edge) -> d NULL -> 500. CORRECTIONS to the hunter claim: (a) the enclosing method is the PRIVATE `salesData` (starts line 1338), NOT `Update` (which is at line 709) â€” the query is reached via the `else` branch at line 1531 for non-Walking/registered customers; (b) the identical unfixed pattern also exists in the sibling `OrderController.cs:915-916` (same `d` DefaultIfEmpty left-join at lines 897-898) and is likewise still a port-break. Severity: medium â€” it 500s only on the edge where a registered-customer SalesEntry has no SEPayment row (mid-save window or orphaned data); in the normal completed flow a SEPayment row exists so it returns fine.
